title: NCERT Ch-2: Understanding the Weather
subject: Social Science (Geography)
instructions: Read the instructions for each section carefully before answering.

questions.text: Which layer of the atmosphere is closest to the Earth's surface and is where almost all weather phenomena take place?
Which instrument is used to measure the speed of the wind?
The normal atmospheric pressure at the sea coast is approximately:
Which scale is commonly used alongside Celsius to measure temperature?
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) was established in which year?
The amount of water vapour present in the air is known as __________ .
An instrument called a __________ is used to measure atmospheric pressure.
Natural balls of ice that fall from the sky are called __________ .
The abbreviation used for the unit of atmospheric pressure is __________ .
Humidity is measured as a percentage, where 100% means the air is saturated with water vapour.
A wind sock is primarily used by farmers to predict the arrival of the monsoon.
Atmospheric pressure increases as one climbs higher up into the mountains.
What does the term ‘Meteorology’ mean?
Name one animal behavior that humans have traditionally used to forecast rain.
Identify the instrument used to measure the depth of rainwater.

sections.title: Section B: Assertion and Reasoning
sections.directions: Directions (Q16–Q20): Select the correct answer from the codes below: (a) Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A. (b) Both A and R are true, but R is NOT the correct explanation of A. (c) A is true, but R is false. (d) A is false, but R is true.

questions.text: Assertion (A): People often feel breathless or dizzy at very high altitudes like Khardung la. Reason (R): At high altitudes, the air pressure is lower, and there is less oxygen available for the lungs.
Assertion (A): Wet clothes dry more slowly on a rainy day. Reason (R): When the humidity in the air is already high, water evaporates more slowly.
Assertion (A): The India Meteorological Department's motto is related to the sun. Reason (R): According to ancient texts like the Manusmṛiti, rain arises from the sun.
Assertion (A): Anemometers are used to determine the direction of the wind. Reason (R): Wind speed is measured in kilometres per hour (km/h) based on how fast the metal cups rotate.
Assertion (A): Pine cones are natural indicators of environmental humidity. Reason (R): Pine cones open in humid conditions to protect their seeds from getting wet.
Explain the difference between 'weather' and 'atmosphere' based on the text. Marks: 2
Differentiate between 'maximum temperature' and 'minimum temperature' in the context of calculating the 'range of temperature.'
Describe why the troposphere is the most significant layer for land-based life.
Why is it important for air pilots and sailors to have accurate wind data?
What is 'relative humidity' and how does it differ in dry versus humid weather?
How does a 'depression' or low-pressure system affect the weather?
Explain why a rain gauge should be placed in an open area away from obstructions.
What are 'Automated Weather Stations' (AWS) and why are they useful in remote areas like glacial lakes?
Briefly explain why the body needs to 'acclimatise' when traveling to high altitudes.
Why do people in many parts of India still value traditional ways of predicting the monsoon?

A traveler is deciding between visiting Jaipur (dry) or Kochi (coastal/humid) in October. Both have a temperature of 30°C. Analyze how the humidity will affect their physical comfort (sweating) and the drying of their laundry. Suggest which city would feel "heavier."

If a meteorologist observes that the atmospheric pressure in a coastal town has dropped from 1013 mb to 990 mb, predict the likely change in weather. Explain the science behind why the local government might issue a warning.

A student records that a city's maximum temperature is 32°C and its minimum is 18°C. Apply the formulas from the text to calculate the range of temperature and the mean daily temperature for that day.
Categorize the following instruments with their specific functions and units of measurement (where applicable): Barometer, Hygrometer, Rain Gauge, Anemometer, Thermometer.
questions.responseLength: Table format or clear categorized list

questions.text: Analyze the weather data for Jammu and Kashmir on 01-02-2024. Compare Srinagar and Gulmarg in terms of maximum temperature and relative humidity. Which station experienced the most significant departure from normal temperature?

Evaluate the different site options for setting up a rain gauge: a school vegetable garden, a building terrace, or an open ground with an elevated platform. Which site is best and why? Compare this with why a verandah would be an unsuitable choice.

An argument is made that "modern technology has made traditional nature-based weather observations obsolete." Evaluate the scientific accuracy of this by discussing how meteorologists use both nature's clues and "cool gadgets" (AWS) to provide early warnings.

Design a Weather Safety Campaign: You are tasked with creating a safety guide for a coastal community expecting a cyclone. Create a list of five key actions or warnings based on IMD protocols to ensure the safety of fishermen and residents.
questions.responseLength: 6–8 sentences arranged in a clear numbered list

questions.text: Create a ‘Weekly Weather Diary’ Structure: Design a logbook page that a student can use to track the five elements of weather daily. Include columns for instruments used and spaces for calculating weekly statistics like mean temperature.
